# Panel

Presents read-only name-value details in responsive sections.

## Installation

Add Panel from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/panel
```

## Usage

Compose a root from `PanelHeader`, `PanelContent`, labeled `PanelItem` rows, and an optional `PanelFooter`. The component supplies spacing and subtle dividers but intentionally leaves the outer surface to its parent.

```vue
<template>
  <Panel class="w-full">
    <PanelHeader>
      <PanelTitle>General</PanelTitle>
    </PanelHeader>
    <PanelContent>
      <PanelItem label="Username">
        peeters
      </PanelItem>
      <PanelItem label="Phone Number">
        +421 950 123 123
      </PanelItem>
    </PanelContent>
  </Panel>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Panel,
  PanelContent,
  PanelHeader,
  PanelItem,
  PanelTitle,
} from '@/Components/Panel'
</script>
```

## Responsive rows

A labeled Panel Item stacks its label above the value by default, then switches to a two-fifths/three-fifths row from the `sm` breakpoint. Values remain in the default slot, so links, badges, and compact controls can be composed without turning the label into markup.

Omit `label` for custom full-width content. The label prop is a string; if a label needs richer markup or a programmatic relationship, build that row explicitly instead of forcing it into Panel Item.

## Semantics

Panel is a visual composition rather than a native description list. Use the root's Reka primitive `as` or `as-child` support to choose an appropriate sectioning element, and make sure the fixed `h3` rendered by `PanelTitle` fits the page heading hierarchy.

## Data and presentation boundary

Pass already formatted values into the rows. Fetching, permissions, localization, date and number formatting, and edit state belong to the owning feature. Use an actual form when values are editable rather than making read-only rows behave like inputs.

## When to use

- Use Panel for profile, account, order, or system metadata with distinct labels.
- Use Card for free-form content with actions and a stronger outer surface.
- Use Table for repeated records whose columns must align across rows.

Hosted documentation: [Panel](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/panel)
